text.title: Pin schema_version literal in every SO model
text.summary: Embed a version literal in every shared Pydantic schema, persist it in every event, route consumers by version with a typed dispatcher.

section.title: The version-literal rule
section.p: A Pydantic model used as a shared contract between pipeline stages MUST declare a `schema_version: Literal["vN"] = "vN"` field. Strict mode forces the model to emit exactly that literal, so the version is part of the typed payload — not a sidecar header that can drift.

rule.statement: For every Pydantic model used as a cross-stage contract, declare `schema_version: Literal["vN"] = "vN"` as the first field, persist it on every emitted event, and route consumers via a `dict[str, type[BaseModel]]` parser registry keyed by version.
rule.rationale: Without an in-payload version, schema drift is silent: old consumers parse new payloads and crash on unrelated fields, masking the actual breaking change; with a typed registry, unknown versions raise at the boundary and old data still parses with old code.

example.note: Backfill jobs run old and new schemas side-by-side; unknown versions fail loud at the parse boundary.

example.code:
class ArticleDraft(BaseModel): body: str title: str tags: list[str]
# stage_2_enrich.py — parses untagged dicts; new producer renames `tags` # to `topics` and old consumer crashes deep inside model_validate. draft = ArticleDraft.model_validate(payload)
example.note: Schema change is invisible until production parse fails; root cause hides under a Pydantic ValidationError on an unrelated field.

antipattern.description: Tracking schema version in a sidecar header (HTTP header, Kafka header, file metadata) outside the payload.
antipattern.reason: Sidecars are stripped by the first naive `payload = response.json()` consumer; in-payload version travels with the data and survives every serialization round-trip.

section.reference: v3 and v4 schemas with typed dispatcher.
Wilkins — SO pipelines 2026, schema versioning section.
CI for LLM prompts — versioned contracts.
